description: Q2 2026 Enhancements — session duration fix, reschedule links, custom/smart durations, auto-archive, and CRM lite

userStories.title: Change session duration default to 7 days and use config value
userStories.description: As a host, I want to stay logged in for 7 days so I don't have to re-authenticate daily.
acceptanceCriteria: SESSION_DURATION_HOURS default changed from 24 to 168 in internal/config/config.go
All http.SetCookie calls in internal/handlers/auth.go use cfg.App.SessionDuration for MaxAge instead of hardcoded 24 * time.Hour
Grep auth.go for all MaxAge occurrences and fix every one (Login, Register, SelectOrg, Google login/signup)
Session record TTL in database matches the configured duration
Existing sessions continue to work after deployment (no breaking schema changes)
Build succeeds

userStories.title: Add reschedule link to confirmation email body
userStories.description: As an invitee, I want a reschedule link in my booking confirmation email so I can change the meeting time.
acceptanceCriteria: Confirmation email body includes a clearly labeled 'Reschedule this meeting' link
Link format: {BASE_URL}/m/{tenant_slug}/{host_slug}/{template_slug}/reschedule/{booking_id}
Link is on its own line, visually distinct from surrounding text
Build succeeds
userStories.notes: Edit internal/services/email.go — the confirmation email template function.

userStories.title: Add reschedule link to ICS calendar event descriptions
userStories.description: As an invitee, I want a reschedule link in my calendar event details so I can find it when viewing the meeting.
acceptanceCriteria: ICS DESCRIPTION field in email attachment includes reschedule URL on its own line, labeled 'Reschedule this meeting:'
Google Calendar API event description also includes the reschedule URL
CalDAV/iCloud event description also includes the reschedule URL
Verify that the existing reschedule page handles cancelled or past bookings gracefully (shows appropriate message, does not error)
Build succeeds
userStories.notes: Three locations: ICS generation in email.go, Google Calendar event in calendar.go, CalDAV event in calendar.go. Also verify FR-203: reschedule page graceful handling of edge cases.

userStories.title: Add reschedule link to reminder email
userStories.description: As an invitee, I want a reschedule link in reminder emails so I can adjust if my schedule changed.
acceptanceCriteria: Reminder email body includes the same reschedule link format as confirmation email
Reminder ICS attachment description includes the reschedule URL
Build succeeds
userStories.notes: Already completed as part of US-002 (defaultReminderBody has reschedule link) and US-003 (generateICS has reschedule URL, called for reminders at line 559).

userStories.title: Add backend validation for custom durations
userStories.description: As a developer, I need backend validation so only valid durations (5-480 minutes) are accepted.
acceptanceCriteria: Template service rejects durations outside the 5-480 minute range with a clear error message
Template service rejects non-integer or negative values
Duplicate durations within a template are deduplicated before save
Durations are sorted ascending before storage
Build succeeds
userStories.notes: Backend-only change in template service. Must be done before UI changes.

userStories.title: Expand preset duration chips in template form
userStories.description: As a host, I want more preset duration options (20, 25, 50, 75, 120 min) in the template form.
acceptanceCriteria: Duration chip options are: 15, 20, 25, 30, 45, 50, 60, 75, 90, 120 minutes
Chips displayed in ascending order
Existing templates with current durations continue to work unchanged
Build succeeds
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Edit templates/pages/dashboard_template_form.html — update the hardcoded chip values.

userStories.title: Add custom duration free-form input to template form
userStories.description: As a host, I want to type a custom meeting duration so I can offer non-standard lengths.
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Custom' input field appears alongside the preset chips
Host can type any integer value between 5 and 480
Invalid values show an inline error message
Custom value is added to the template durations array alongside selected presets
If custom value matches a preset, no duplicate is created
Build succeeds
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: UI change in templates/pages/dashboard_template_form.html. Backend validation already in place from US-005.

userStories.title: Add smart_durations column to hosts table
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a database column to store the smart durations preference per host.
acceptanceCriteria: New migration adds boolean column smart_durations to hosts table with default false
Migration works with both SQLite and PostgreSQL
Host model struct in internal/models/models.go includes SmartDurations bool field
Build succeeds
userStories.notes: Schema-only change. Create a new migration file in migrations/ directory.

userStories.title: Add smart durations toggle to settings page
userStories.description: As a host, I want a toggle on my settings page to enable smart durations.
acceptanceCriteria: Toggle labeled 'Smart durations' on the Settings page
Helper text: 'End meetings 5-10 minutes early to give you buffer time'
Toggle saves the smart_durations value to the host record via UpdateSettings handler
Build succeeds
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Edit templates/pages/dashboard_settings.html and the UpdateSettings handler.

userStories.title: Apply smart duration logic at booking creation
userStories.description: As a system, I need to shave time off meeting end times when smart durations is enabled.
acceptanceCriteria: When host has smart_durations enabled: durations <= 30 min subtract 5 min, durations > 30 min subtract 10 min
Minimum resulting duration is 5 minutes (never goes to 0)
Adjusted duration is used for booking end_time calculation
Adjusted duration is used for ICS DTEND, Google Calendar end time, and CalDAV end time
Original unadjusted duration is stored on the booking record for display
Invitee booking page still shows the original duration (e.g. '30 min' not '25 min')
Build succeeds
userStories.notes: Apply in booking service layer. Formula: actual = duration <= 30 ? duration - 5 : duration - 10; actual = max(actual, 5). Note: shares ICS/calendar code paths with US-003 (reschedule links) — US-003 runs first.

userStories.title: Apply smart duration to rescheduled bookings
userStories.description: As a system, I need smart durations applied consistently when bookings are rescheduled.
acceptanceCriteria: Rescheduled booking end_time uses the same smart duration logic as new bookings
Uses the host's current smart_durations setting at reschedule time
Build succeeds
userStories.notes: Update the RescheduleBooking method in booking service.

userStories.title: Remove status restriction on booking archival
userStories.description: As a host, I want to archive any past booking regardless of its status.
acceptanceCriteria: ArchiveBooking service method allows archiving any booking whose end_time is in the past
Future bookings with status confirmed or pending cannot be archived
Manual archive button works for individual past confirmed bookings
Build succeeds
userStories.notes: Modify ArchiveBooking in internal/services/booking.go — change the status check to a time check.

userStories.title: Add ArchiveOldBookings repository method
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a bulk archive query that archives bookings older than a cutoff time.
acceptanceCriteria: New repository method: ArchiveOldBookings(ctx, cutoffTime) returns (count int, err error)
Archives all unarchived bookings where end_time < cutoffTime, across all tenants
Uses q() helper for SQLite/Postgres compatibility
Build succeeds
userStories.notes: Add to booking repository. Used by both the background job and the dashboard button.

userStories.title: Add 'Archive all past' button to bookings dashboard
userStories.description: As a host, I want a button to archive all past meetings at once.
acceptanceCriteria: 'Archive all past' button visible on the bookings dashboard page
Button calls ArchiveOldBookings with now as cutoff (archives everything past)
Button is hidden when there are no past unarchived bookings
Shows confirmation with count before archiving (e.g. 'Archive 12 past meetings?')
Build succeeds
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Add button to dashboard template and a new handler endpoint.

userStories.title: Add automatic archival background worker
userStories.description: As a system, I need to auto-archive bookings older than 2 weeks on a daily schedule.
acceptanceCriteria: Background goroutine starts on application boot in main.go
Runs on a 24-hour ticker
Calls ArchiveOldBookings with now minus 14 days as cutoff
Logs the number of bookings archived per run
Graceful shutdown: stops when application context is cancelled
Build succeeds
userStories.notes: Add worker goroutine in cmd/server/main.go. Use context.Context and time.Ticker.

userStories.title: Create contacts table and model
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a contacts data model to store deduplicated invitee information.
acceptanceCriteria: New migration creates contacts table with: id (UUID PK), tenant_id (FK), name (text not null), email (text not null), phone (text nullable), timezone (text nullable), first_met (timestamp), last_met (timestamp), meeting_count (integer default 0), created_at, updated_at
Unique constraint on (tenant_id, email)
Index on tenant_id for list queries
Index on email for lookup
Migration works with both SQLite and PostgreSQL
Go model struct added to internal/models/models.go
Build succeeds

userStories.title: Create contact repository with Upsert and List methods
userStories.description: As a developer, I need repository methods to create/update and list contacts.
acceptanceCriteria: Upsert method: INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (tenant_id, email) DO UPDATE — creates or updates a contact atomically
List method: returns contacts for a tenant with optional search filter (name or email), offset, and limit, sorted by last_met descending
GetByEmail method: returns a single contact by (tenantID, email) for lookup
GetBookings method: returns bookings for a given contact email within a tenant
Uses q() helper for SQLite/Postgres placeholder conversion
Build succeeds

userStories.title: Create contact service and hook into booking confirmation
userStories.description: As a system, I need to auto-create/update contacts when bookings are confirmed.
acceptanceCriteria: Contact service with UpsertFromBooking method that extracts name/email/phone/timezone from a booking
Called from booking service when a booking is confirmed (new or approved)
If no contact exists: creates with first_met and last_met set to booking start_time, meeting_count = 1
If contact exists: updates last_met if later, increments meeting_count, fills empty name/phone/timezone
Contact errors are logged but do not block or fail the booking flow
Build succeeds
userStories.notes: Create internal/services/contact.go. Wire into booking service confirmation flow.

userStories.title: Add contacts list page to dashboard
userStories.description: As a host, I want a contacts page showing all people I've met with.
acceptanceCriteria: New dashboard page at /dashboard/contacts
Navigation link added to the dashboard sidebar/nav
Displays table with columns: Name, Email, Meetings (count), Last Met (relative date)
Sorted by last_met descending
Search input filters by name or email (HTMX-powered with hx-get and debounce)
Empty state message when no contacts exist
Build succeeds
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Create handler, register route, create template. Follow existing dashboard page patterns.

userStories.title: Add contact meeting history drill-down
userStories.description: As a host, I want to click a contact to see their meeting history.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking a contact row expands inline or navigates to show booking history for that contact
Shows bookings: date, template name, status, duration
Sorted by date descending
Build succeeds
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Use HTMX hx-get to load a partial with the booking list for the contact.

userStories.title: Backfill contacts from existing bookings
userStories.description: As a host, I want existing confirmed bookings to populate contacts so I don't start from zero.
acceptanceCriteria: Backfill mechanism processes all confirmed bookings and creates/updates contact records
Runs on first access to contacts page if contacts table is empty for the tenant, or as a manual trigger
Idempotent: running multiple times produces the same result
Build succeeds
userStories.notes: Can be a service method called from the contacts handler on first load.
